The question

Customers hire products to meet a need.
Is it doing its job? When it’s not — what’s the impact?

The reasoning chain

someone depends on this and has an expectation of what “working” means
if that breaks there is a consequence — to whom, how severe
so we need to know a measurable condition — not “it feels healthy” but a number with a threshold
detected by telemetry the system already produces
creates a signal — measurement, threshold, owner, next action
informs a dashboard and a runbook — current state against target, what to do when it fires

The chain works the same way for every service. What changes is the answers.

Who does this work

A requirements engineer who:

  • decomposes stakeholder expectations into measurable needs
  • walks a structured chain for each one — what breaks, what to measure, what good looks like

An observability practitioner who:

  • designs signals across business, process, and system layers
  • builds diagnostic paths — when something degrades, where to look

A service analyst who:

  • reads architecture, runbooks, incident history
  • maps where the service sits in business processes
  • checks coverage — is every expectation measured

The instrument brings all three together in one pass. Where it’s confident, it tells you. Where it’s guessing, it tells you that too.

Extracted source said it · Inferred reasonable conclusion · Constructed best guess, needs validation

Splunk Cloud Enterprise Loggingmetadata.display_name

First-pass draft. Every value below comes from the service understanding document. Toggle “Show field sources” to see which document field produced each value.

What this service is

Identity — straight from the service profile. The reasoning is below.

What it is
Enterprise log platform — 1.1M searches/day serving 5,000 users across all lines of business for incident troubleshooting.service.business_purpose_display

Why these signals — the reasoning, stakeholder by stakeholder

Each thread is the chain the instrument walked: who cares → what they expect → what we need to know → how we'd know → what's at risk.

Platform Operations Operational Criticalstakeholders[].name
expects Proactive platform health detection and remediation Extractedstakeholders[].expectations[].display_text
if unmet Crisis mode — phone calls replace dashboards, adding ~1 hour to incident response.stakeholders[].impact_description_display
so we need to know Can we detect all categories of platform health issues?needs[].display_name
Proactive detection requires covering all categories of degradation — hardware faults, pipeline saturation, search performance, and cluster instability. One need, but verified by multiple signals because no single metric captures platform health.need_expectations[].decomposition_rationale
good looks like Data freshness, alert reliability, search speed, and queue rate all reporting continuously. Indexer health, pipeline backlog, and cluster stability monitored as leading indicators. Detection before consumers report degradation.needs[].scenario.response_measure
we verify it with Log data arriving within 30 seconds — are dashboards and alerts working with fresh data? — Target: 99.9% Extractedneed_signals[role=outcome] -> signals[].business_description_display + slo_target
we verify it with Scheduled searches executing reliably — are our automated alerts and reports running? — Target: 99.9% Extractedneed_signals[role=outcome] -> signals[].business_description_display + slo_target
we verify it with Troubleshooting queries completing under 20 seconds — at 90%, most sessions hit a wall. — Target: 98% Extractedneed_signals[role=outcome] -> signals[].business_description_display + slo_target
we verify it with Searches starting immediately — at 88%, over 130K searches per day wait in queue. — Target: 99% Extractedneed_signals[role=outcome] -> signals[].business_description_display + slo_target
if it's failing, we check Indexers actually completing searches — catches the 'alive but not serving' gap that caused the May 5th cascade. · Indexing pipeline keeping up — a growing backlog predicts 18-hour shutdowns and data risk. · Cluster stability — a rebuild spike is the early warning signal before search degrades. · Indexer fleet health — at 239 bare-metal machines, hardware degradation is a when, not an if.need_signals[role=diagnostic] -> signals[].business_description_display
if it stays broken, the impact is Operational (minutes_added/incident)needs[].impacts[].category, .unit
⚠ but a required condition is NOT met Per-indexer search health monitoring is deployed and reportingneeds[].preconditions[current_state=violated]
⚠ but a required condition is NOT met Flush backlog is monitored continuously, not only during shutdownneeds[].preconditions[current_state=violated]
⚠ but a required condition is NOT met Bucket map rebuild rate is monitored as a rate signalneeds[].preconditions[current_state=violated]
LOB Engineering Teams Customer Experience Criticalstakeholders[].name
expects 15-minute troubleshooting window — search works when you need it Extractedstakeholders[].expectations[].display_text
if unmet Five LOBs at a vendor engineering session — platform instability now directly impacts their incident response.stakeholders[].impact_description_display
so we need to know Do troubleshooting queries complete within the SLA window?needs[].display_name
The 15-minute troubleshooting window requires two things to be true: queries complete fast enough, and queries start immediately. This need covers the first — are results coming back within 20 seconds?need_expectations[].decomposition_rationale
good looks like Ad-hoc search runtime compliance >= 98% over 7d rolling windowneeds[].scenario.response_measure
we verify it with Troubleshooting queries completing under 20 seconds — at 90%, most sessions hit a wall. — Target: 98% Extractedneed_signals[role=outcome] -> signals[].business_description_display + slo_target
if it's failing, we check Indexers actually completing searches — catches the 'alive but not serving' gap that caused the May 5th cascade. · Cluster stability — a rebuild spike is the early warning signal before search degrades. · Indexer fleet health — at 239 bare-metal machines, hardware degradation is a when, not an if.need_signals[role=diagnostic] -> signals[].business_description_display
if it stays broken, the impact is Customer Experienceneeds[].impacts[].category, .unit
⚠ but a required condition is NOT met Parsing job bug is resolved (currently adding 7-15 seconds to ALL searches)needs[].preconditions[current_state=violated]
⚠ but a required condition is NOT met CT SHC is split from Ad-hoc SHC (Priority 1, target 6/30)needs[].preconditions[current_state=violated]
also matters to Platform Operationsneed_stakeholders[]
so we need to know Can engineers search immediately when an incident occurs?needs[].display_name
The second part of the 15-minute window — even fast queries don't help if you're waiting in a queue to run them. At 12% queuing, engineers lose minutes before they even start troubleshooting.need_expectations[].decomposition_rationale
good looks like Ad-hoc search immediate availability >= 99% over 7d rolling windowneeds[].scenario.response_measure
we verify it with Searches starting immediately — at 88%, over 130K searches per day wait in queue. — Target: 99% Extractedneed_signals[role=outcome] -> signals[].business_description_display + slo_target
if it's failing, we check Indexers actually completing searches — catches the 'alive but not serving' gap that caused the May 5th cascade.need_signals[role=diagnostic] -> signals[].business_description_display
if it stays broken, the impact is Customer Experienceneeds[].impacts[].category, .unit
⚠ but a required condition is NOT met CT SHC split reduces shared search head cluster contentionneeds[].preconditions[current_state=violated]
⚠ but a required condition is NOT met Per-indexer concurrency enables per-node capacity allocation instead of shared poolneeds[].preconditions[current_state=violated]
also matters to LOB End Usersneed_stakeholders[]
LOB End Users Customer Experience Highstakeholders[].name
expects Responsive search and dashboard experience Extractedstakeholders[].expectations[].display_text
if unmet 10-20% chance of being queued, 10% chance of 20+ second waits — refresh amplification makes it worse.stakeholders[].impact_description_display
so we need to know Do searches and dashboards respond within acceptable time?needs[].display_name
End users judge the platform by one thing: does it respond when I use it? Search speed and queue rate are the same measurements that matter for LOB engineers, but the reason is different — engineers need it for incidents, end users need it for daily work.need_expectations[].decomposition_rationale
good looks like Search runtime compliance >= 98% AND immediate availability >= 99%needs[].scenario.response_measure
we verify it with Troubleshooting queries completing under 20 seconds — at 90%, most sessions hit a wall. — Target: 98% Extractedneed_signals[role=outcome] -> signals[].business_description_display + slo_target
we verify it with Searches starting immediately — at 88%, over 130K searches per day wait in queue. — Target: 99% Extractedneed_signals[role=outcome] -> signals[].business_description_display + slo_target
if it stays broken, the impact is Customer Experienceneeds[].impacts[].category, .unit
⚠ but a required condition is NOT met A consumer-facing transparency mechanism exists to communicate degradationneeds[].preconditions[current_state=violated]
Executive Leadership Financial Mediumstakeholders[].name
expects Quantified SLO compliance and investment ROI Inferredstakeholders[].expectations[].display_text
if unmet Year-of-value deferred, migration ROI negative, operational risk management actively engaged.stakeholders[].impact_description_display
so we need to know Are SLOs quantified with error budgets and compliance tracking?needs[].display_name
Leadership needs data-backed answers about platform health. Today four KPIs exist with color-coded thresholds, but no formal SLO definitions, no error budgets, and no compliance tracking. You can't measure what you haven't defined.need_expectations[].decomposition_rationale
good looks like Formal SLO definitions exist with error budgets and compliance trackingneeds[].scenario.response_measure
if it's failing, we check Troubleshooting queries completing under 20 seconds — at 90%, most sessions hit a wall.need_signals[role=diagnostic] -> signals[].business_description_display
if it stays broken, the impact is Financialneeds[].impacts[].category, .unit
⚠ but a required condition is NOT met Formal SLO/SLI definitions exist (none exist today)needs[].preconditions[current_state=violated]
Downstream Banking Customers Customer Experience Mediumstakeholders[].name
expects Incident resolution not prolonged by log platform degradation Inferredstakeholders[].expectations[].display_text
if unmet Splunk's 90-minute degradation extends every concurrent LOB incident — downstream customer impact unquantified.stakeholders[].impact_description_display
so we need to know Does Splunk degradation stay contained so LOB incidents don't extend?needs[].display_name
Banking customers don't use Splunk, but they feel it when it breaks — Splunk degradation extends every concurrent LOB incident. The same search speed and queue rate that matter for LOB engineers determine whether downstream customers wait longer.need_expectations[].decomposition_rationale
good looks like MTTR amplification during Splunk degradation is quantified and within acceptable boundsneeds[].scenario.response_measure
we verify it with Troubleshooting queries completing under 20 seconds — at 90%, most sessions hit a wall. — Target: 98% Extractedneed_signals[role=outcome] -> signals[].business_description_display + slo_target
we verify it with Searches starting immediately — at 88%, over 130K searches per day wait in queue. — Target: 99% Extractedneed_signals[role=outcome] -> signals[].business_description_display + slo_target
if it stays broken, the impact is Customer Experience (affected_lobs/incident)needs[].impacts[].category, .unit
⚠ but a required condition is NOT met Per-LOB search experience decomposition exists to assess differential impactneeds[].preconditions[current_state=violated]
⚠ but a required condition is NOT met MTTR amplification is measured (currently unquantified)needs[].preconditions[current_state=violated]
also matters to LOB Engineering Teamsneed_stakeholders[]

Prior art referenced in this analysis

Grouped by the expertise that uses them — the same three roles from the Framework tab.

Requirements engineer

FrameworkWhat it isWhat it does here
ArchiMate Motivation Extension Part of the ArchiMate enterprise architecture standard. Models why an organization does what it does — stakeholders, their goals, and what drives requirements. Structures the reasoning chain — stakeholder → expectation → requirement → measurable need.
GQM (Goal-Question-Metric) Measurement framework from software engineering research. Start with a goal, derive questions, then identify metrics that answer them. The decomposition pattern — a business goal becomes a question (“is this sub-capability healthy?”) which becomes a measurable signal.
Meyer Design by Contract Software design principle where components specify what they require and what they guarantee. Frames expectations as contracts — each one has a condition that can be verified and a consequence when it’s violated.
SEI Quality Attribute Scenarios Structured format from the Software Engineering Institute for describing quality requirements — stimulus, environment, response, measure. Shapes how needs are written — what triggers it, what the expected behavior is, how to measure whether it’s met.

Observability practitioner

FrameworkWhat it isWhat it does here
APQC Process Classification Industry-standard taxonomy for business processes. Common vocabulary for how work gets done across organizations. Classifies business health signals by dimension — effectiveness, efficiency, adaptability — so each signal measures a distinct aspect of whether a capability is doing its job.
Google Golden Signals Four categories of metrics — latency, traffic, errors, saturation — from the Google SRE handbook. Widely adopted for monitoring distributed systems. Classifies process-layer signals. Where APQC measures business outcomes, Golden Signals measure the operational behaviors that produce them.
SRE SLO Practice Site Reliability Engineering approach to service level objectives. Defines how to set measurable targets for service behavior. Shapes every signal — each one has a measurement, a threshold, an owner, and a defined response when the threshold is breached.
SLODLC (SLO Development Lifecycle) Lifecycle model for developing service level objectives — discover, define, implement, operate, refine. Positions this analysis in the Discover phase — identifying what to measure before committing to targets or building dashboards.
Generated from BOS onboarding · for review, not a validated record.
